suggested_titles.title: Crimson Forms on Verdant Field
suggested_titles.subtitle: An abstract interplay of color and structure
suggested_titles.description: A bold composition featuring stark red geometric forms against a deep, textured green ground, punctuated by contrasting blues and blacks.

suggested_titles.title: Abstract Strata: Green and Red
suggested_titles.subtitle: Textural abstraction evoking landscape or architectural elements
suggested_titles.description: Dynamic impasto strokes create a sense of depth and tension through vibrant reds and blues set against a dominant, rich green.

suggested_titles.title: Architectonic Green Red
suggested_titles.subtitle: A study in contrasting color and form
suggested_titles.description: Strong, block-like red figures emerge from a profound green background, anchored by a dark, grounded element and subtle horizontal lines.

contextual_proposals.context_role: Showcases the artwork's scale and physical presence within a pristine, contemporary exhibition space, emphasizing its sculptural quality and commanding stature from an art-focused, low perspective.
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: Calm, clean, and architecturally precise, with an emphasis on verticality and depth conveyed through a dramatically low vantage point, allowing the artwork to assert its commanding presence.
materials: Matte mineral white walls with subtle texture
Simple white ceiling with discreet recessed track lighting
Minimalist white architectural plinth or low bench (partially visible)
contextual_proposals.lighting: Soft, cool gallery daylight entering from an unseen overhead skylight, supplemented by discreet, focused track lighting highlighting the artwork's texture and color, creating subtle shadows that define depth and dimension.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Nadir Controlado: Camera is positioned very low, near the floor, approximately 20-35 cm from the ground, using a controlled nadir-inspired architectural viewpoint. The floor plane, wall height, ceiling lines, columns, door frames or architectural verticals may rise strongly in the composition. Use a controlled oblique angle across the room, not a straight-on product view, with one coherent vanishing direction for architecture and artwork. Use the nadir effect mainly through the surrounding room geometry: floor lines, vertical wall planes, ceiling height, architectural edges, columns, furniture legs or door frames. The artwork remains the visual subject, but the dramatic low viewpoint is carried by the architecture rather than by warping the canvas.
contextual_proposals.camera_distance: Controlled architectural distance, allowing for a strong foreground floor presence and prominent rising architectural lines, while keeping the artwork visually dominant and physically imposing.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The extremely low camera position emphasizes the artwork's scale and physical presence, making it appear monumental and grounded yet aspirational. The controlled oblique angle introduces dynamic spatial tension, revealing the physical thickness of the canvas edge and the deep perspective of the gallery architecture. The rising vertical lines of the wall and a subtle architectural element (like a low plinth or bench) in the foreground guide the eye upwards to the artwork, asserting its central and powerful role.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: The artwork's bold, almost architectural forms and strong color contrasts are perfectly complemented by the pristine, minimalist gallery setting. The clean lines and neutral palette of the environment allow the artwork's intense green and vibrant red elements to truly resonate, highlighting its abstract monumentality and expressive impasto. The low camera angle enhances the sense of the artwork as a grounded yet powerfully ascending structure.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: This setup positions the artwork as a significant collector's piece suitable for high-end contemporary spaces and institutional collections. The pristine gallery environment communicates value, prestige, and serious artistic merit. The dynamic, low camera perspective adds visual interest and memorability, making the artwork stand out in a competitive market by emphasizing its physical presence and substantiality as a tangible art object.
contextual_proposals.mockup_prompt: The artwork (from reference image) is a large, vertical, rectangular abstract painting dominated by a deep green field with bold red and blue geometric forms and impasto textures. It is installed centrally on a pristine, matte mineral white gallery wall, appearing elevated and dominant. The environment is a minimalist contemporary art gallery. The wall has a subtle texture and no signage. The floor is polished light gray concrete, extending prominently into the foreground. A simple, low white architectural plinth or bench is positioned partially cropped in the extreme foreground, its lines rising subtly into view. The ceiling is a simple white plane with discreet recessed track lighting. Controlled, soft, cool gallery daylight from an unseen overhead source gently illuminates the space, while focused track lights cast subtle, crisp highlights on the artwork's texture and colors. The scene is captured from a very low vantage point, near the floor. The polished concrete floor plane rises strongly from the foreground. The pristine matte white wall with the artwork creates strong vertical lines. Architectural edges and the low plinth define clear vanishing lines towards the artwork. The composition shows a controlled oblique view across the room, with coherent vanishing lines for all architectural elements, emphasizing depth and making the artwork appear dominant and elevated. Artwork scale is perfectly preserved, physically plausible, and dominant. No visible people, no animals, no generic vintage room, no warm rustic palette.
contextual_proposals.negative_prompt: no people, no animals, no poster, no flat print, no distortion, no fisheye, no incorrect scale, no cropped artwork, no altered artwork, no mirrored artwork, no generic vintage room, no winter default unless seasonally intended.
contextual_proposals.camera_first_camera_view: Nadir Controlado: Camera is positioned very low, near the floor, approximately 20-35 cm from the ground, using a controlled nadir-inspired architectural viewpoint. The floor plane, wall height, ceiling lines, columns, door frames or architectural verticals may rise strongly in the composition. Use a controlled oblique angle across the room, not a straight-on product view, with one coherent vanishing direction for architecture and artwork. Use the nadir effect mainly through the surrounding room geometry: floor lines, vertical wall planes, ceiling height, architectural edges, columns, furniture legs or door frames. The artwork remains the visual subject, but the dramatic low viewpoint is carried by the architecture rather than by warping the canvas.

contextual_proposals.context_role: To present the artwork as a cherished, culturally significant piece within an intimate and thoughtful private collection, emphasizing its substantial physical presence through dramatic perspective.
contextual_proposals.space_type: Private collector's study or quiet living room
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: Intimate, silent, culturally literate, with an air of sophisticated contemplation and a warm, inviting evening glow.
materials: Muted warm limewash walls in a soft ochre-grey
low-slung dark wenge wood console table
contextual_proposals.lighting: Focused, warm evening lighting from a hidden overhead source or a refined floor lamp, casting subtle shadows and making the artwork's colors glow, complemented by a soft, diffused ambient light from a window treated with sheer linen.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Contrapicado 7/8: Camera height is low, below normal knee height, with the viewpoint grounded close to the floor but still architectural. Use a 7/8 oblique rotation so the wall, artwork depth, and room volume are visible through one shared perspective. The artwork is seen with strong side depth and believable thickness; use clear asymmetry and negative space toward the vanishing side while keeping surrounding wall and floor aligned to the same camera.
contextual_proposals.camera_distance: Controlled architectural distance, allowing the artwork to be the dominant feature while establishing the depth of the room.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The low, oblique angle dramatically emphasizes the artwork's verticality and physical presence, making it feel monumental and deeply integrated into the sophisticated space. It highlights the canvas's real thickness and creates a dynamic, engaging composition that draws the viewer into the room, elevating its perceived importance.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: This intimate collector's den, with its muted, warm palette and refined materials, provides a serene and sophisticated backdrop that allows the artwork's bold abstract forms and vibrant colors to command attention and resonate deeply. The quiet setting elevates the artwork's status as a central cultural object, aligning perfectly with the 'Domestic Collector World' directive.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: Positions the artwork as a substantial, impactful investment for a discerning collector. The dramatic camera angle and refined, intimate setting enhance its perceived value and desirability, showcasing its ability to anchor and enrich a premium private residence, appealing to a market that appreciates art as a cultural centerpiece.
contextual_proposals.mockup_prompt: A sophisticated private collector's quiet living room with muted warm limewash walls in an ochre-grey and dark wide-plank oak flooring. The artwork (insert exact artwork here, full size, high detail, physically accurate, original colors, original textures, no modifications) is hung on the wall, acting as the cultural centerpiece. A low-slung dark wenge wood console table is partially visible in the near foreground, providing subtle depth without obscuring the artwork. A deep charcoal wool rug is partially visible on the floor, adding texture. The camera is low (below knee height) with an upward tilt and a 7/8 oblique rotation, showing strong side depth of the artwork and the room's volume. Warm evening lighting from a hidden source makes the artwork's vibrant red and blue glow against the subtle background, complemented by soft, diffused ambient light from a window with sheer linen in the background. No people, no animals.
contextual_proposals.negative_prompt: no people, no animals, no poster, no flat print, no distortion, no fisheye, no incorrect scale, no cropped artwork, no altered artwork, no mirrored artwork, no generic vintage room, no winter default unless seasonally intended, no bright harsh light, no overwhelming decor, no busy patterns, no hotel catalog, no furniture showroom.
contextual_proposals.camera_first_camera_view: Contrapicado 7/8: Camera height is low, below normal knee height, with the viewpoint grounded close to the floor but still architectural. Use a 7/8 oblique rotation so the wall, artwork depth, and room volume are visible through one shared perspective. The artwork is seen with strong side depth and believable thickness; use clear asymmetry and negative space toward the vanishing side while keeping surrounding wall and floor aligned to the same camera.

contextual_proposals.context_role: To present the artwork in an aspirational, refined residential setting that emphasizes its contemplative depth and bold color contrast within a context of understated luxury and sophisticated domesticity, suitable for premium art collectors.
contextual_proposals.space_type: A spacious, quiet luxury living room within a contemporary palazzo-style residence.
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: A serene, elegant, and warm atmosphere, bathed in the soft, golden glow of late afternoon sun, reflecting off polished surfaces. Restrained luxury with a focus on natural light and noble materials. The feeling is calm, cultivated, and timelessly modern.
materials: Muted cream linen upholstery on armchair
contextual_proposals.lighting: Soft, golden late afternoon daylight entering from large, tall windows on the left side, creating a warm glow and gentle, elongated shadows. Ambient fill from diffused ceiling light, subtly highlighting artwork textures.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Reflejo Dorado de Tarde / Palazzo: Camera height is elegant human standing height, approximately 150-170 cm, as if observed calmly from inside a palazzo room. Use a soft three-quarter rotation that catches reflected late light while keeping the artwork plane legible. The composition is stable, warm, and architectural, with golden afternoon reflection supporting the room rather than repainting the artwork; keep elegant balance but avoid perfect symmetry unless the room itself naturally demands it.
contextual_proposals.camera_distance: Controlled architectural distance, showcasing the artwork as the focal point within the room's elegant proportions.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The subtle three-quarter angle reveals the physical depth and thickness of the canvas, preventing a flat appearance. The perspective creates a sense of spatial volume, drawing the viewer into the refined environment while maintaining the artwork's prominence. The viewpoint from a standing height offers a natural and accessible presentation, emphasizing stability and architectural grace.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: The artwork's deep green and vibrant red resonate beautifully with the warm, earthy tones of limewash and travertine, while the golden afternoon light enhances its inherent warmth and drama. The minimalist composition of the artwork finds harmony with the clean lines and negative space of the quiet luxury interior. The setting amplifies the artwork's contemplative nature, allowing its bold forms to breathe and command attention without visual clutter. The 'palazzo' context suggested by the camera complements the artwork's quiet grandeur and sophisticated simplicity.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: This context positions the artwork as a sophisticated, high-value piece for discerning collectors who appreciate refined domesticity and contemporary elegance. The aspirational setting showcases how the artwork elevates a premium living space, appealing to a market that values understated luxury and thoughtful design. The warm, inviting light makes the artwork feel approachable and desirable for a home environment, while the architectural depth adds prestige and commercial appeal.
contextual_proposals.mockup_prompt: A spacious, quiet luxury living room within a contemporary palazzo-style residence. The walls are creamy limewash plaster with subtle texture. The floor is made of large format pale travertine tiles. A clean, high ceiling. The artwork, featuring a dominant red vertical form against a deep green background with blue and black accents, is hung on the left wall of the room, seen in a soft three-quarter perspective. Below the artwork, a low-slung, dark brushed oak architectural console is present in the midground. A single, minimalist contemporary armchair with muted cream linen upholstery is subtly placed in the foreground, creating architectural depth and a coherent vanishing line. The room is bathed in soft, golden late afternoon daylight entering from tall windows on the left, casting gentle, elongated shadows and highlighting textures. The artwork maintains its exact colors, proportions, and textures. No people, no animals.

Use a soft three-quarter rotation that catches reflected late light while keeping the artwork plane legible. The composition is stable, warm, and architectural, with golden afternoon reflection supporting the room rather than repainting the artwork; keep elegant balance but avoid perfect symmetry unless the room itself naturally demands it.

contextual_proposals.context_role: This context positions the artwork within a timeless European heritage setting, emphasizing its gravitas and ability to harmonise with refined architectural history while retaining its contemporary edge.
contextual_proposals.space_type: Grand Salon in an old European Palazzo
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: A serene, refined historical elegance, contemplative and bathed in warm, golden late afternoon light. The atmosphere evokes sophisticated dialogue between old-world architecture and striking modern abstraction.
materials: Aged limewash walls in warm off-white/cream
Wide plank herringbone parquet floor with subtle patina
Tall arch-top windows with elegant, restrained classical molding
Subtle, barely visible ornate ceiling medallion
Minimalist long console table in dark, polished wood or bronze
contextual_proposals.lighting: Golden late afternoon sunlight streams through tall, unadorned windows, casting soft, elongated shadows across the parquet floor and subtly illuminating the artwork's textures. The room feels warm and inviting, with a distinct contrast between sunlit areas and gently shaded architectural elements.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Vista Aérea con Contexto de Ventanas: Camera height is elevated above normal eye level, high enough to read the room, windows, floor plane, and artwork together. Use a gentle diagonal rotation so window context and the artwork wall are both visible without splitting perspectives. The room is comprehensible from above, with window light and floor geometry giving context while the artwork remains identifiable; allow a moderate offset so floor, windows, and surrounding architecture carry part of the frame.
contextual_proposals.camera_distance: Controlled architectural distance, allowing the artwork to be central while showcasing the room's elegant proportions and window context.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The elevated viewpoint provides a comprehensive, spatial view of the grand palazzo room, with the artwork prominently displayed on a tall, aged plaster wall. The gentle diagonal angle clearly reveals the polished parquet floor extending into the foreground, the grand scale of the room, and the source of the warm late light through large windows, demonstrating the artwork's sophisticated integration into a historically rich architectural environment. The physical thickness and presence of the canvas are subtly discernible.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: The artwork's bold, abstract geometry and rich, deep green, vibrant red, and dark blue tones find a compelling counterpoint in the classic proportions and warm, aged materials of the palazzo. This historical context enriches the artwork by imbuing it with a sense of timelessness and gravitas, demonstrating its ability to transcend eras. The artwork's deep green resonates with the muted historical palette, while the vibrant red offers a striking modern intervention, creating visual tension and intellectual interest.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: This setting positions the artwork as a significant piece suitable for discerning collectors with grand, architecturally rich homes and a sophisticated appreciation for art that bridges history and modernity. It showcases the artwork's versatility and enduring appeal, suggesting it can elevate a historically significant space without being consumed by it. The warm, inviting light and premium materials make the environment desirable, and the elevated camera view offers an aspirational perspective, emphasizing the artwork's prestige and potential for impactful placement.
contextual_proposals.mockup_prompt: An old European palazzo grand salon, viewed from an elevated camera angle looking down at a slight diagonal. A large abstract painting [artwork] hangs prominently on a tall, aged limewash wall in a warm off-white hue. The camera's gentle diagonal rotation reveals a wide plank herringbone parquet floor extending into the foreground, the artwork wall, and tall arch-top windows with restrained classical molding to the side, allowing warm golden late afternoon sunlight to stream in. The light casts soft, elongated shadows on the parquet floor and subtly illuminates the artwork's textures. A minimalist, long, dark polished wood console table sits against the wall beneath the artwork, and a simple antique stone bench is placed in the midground, providing clear compositional geometry on the floor plane. The room features subtle ornate ceiling medallions barely visible in the upper frame. The artwork appears as a physical stretched canvas with discernible thickness. No visible people, animals, or distractions. The room feels spacious and elegantly quiet, with pronounced architectural gravity and rich textures.

contextual_proposals.context_role: To present the artwork in a refined, working artist's environment, emphasizing its creation context and material authenticity, while showcasing its potential in a spacious, architecturally considered setting.
contextual_proposals.space_type: Clean, premium artist's atelier or studio passage.
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: Serene, productive, contemplative, with a focus on material integrity and the creative process, but with a clean, uncluttered presentation. Subtle northern light.
materials: Painted plaster wall (off-white or very light warm gray)
Simple wooden studio easel (discreetly placed or folded)
contextual_proposals.lighting: Soft, consistent north light streaming from a large window, creating gentle shadows and illuminating the artwork without harsh reflections. Ambient studio daylight.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Pasillo, Obra Descentrada y Próxima: Camera height is natural standing or slightly lowered human height, close enough to feel the corridor edge. Use an offset three-quarter corridor angle, with the artwork deliberately off-center and close rather than symmetrically framed. Place the artwork on a long wall plane rather than a short frontal display wall. The wall should extend laterally into the scene like a gallery passage, corridor, or elongated architectural plane, creating a clear vanishing direction. Use floor lines, ceiling lines, wall joints, lighting tracks, windows, columns, doorways, or distant wall planes to reinforce depth. The artwork may sit off-center and relatively close to the viewer, while the architecture recedes naturally to one side. Avoid product-style centered symmetry.
contextual_proposals.camera_distance: Medium-close. The artwork is prominent, but the architectural depth of the studio passage is clearly visible, receding into the background.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The offset three-quarter view highlights the canvas's physical presence and thickness, making it feel like a tangible object within the working studio. The receding wall and floor lines emphasize spatial depth and integrate the artwork into a coherent architectural passage, avoiding a flat, poster-like look. The natural standing height camera feels grounded.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: The artwork, with its bold abstract forms and clear lines, resonates with the clean yet authentic aesthetic of a quiet atelier. Placing it in an artist's studio context, especially one that is refined and well-lit, pays homage to its origin and the creative process, adding a layer of authenticity and intellectual value. The artwork's deep green finds a subtle harmony with the natural, diffused light of a studio environment.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: This context offers a sophisticated and authentic narrative for the artwork. It positions the piece not just as a decorative item but as a product of thoughtful creation, appealing to collectors who value artistic integrity and the story behind the art. The clean studio environment elevates the artwork's professional presentation, making it suitable for high-end art markets. The 'pasillo' view offers a dynamic and commercially engaging visual.
contextual_proposals.mockup_prompt: A large abstract painting, predominantly green with bold red and subtle blue/black geometric forms, is hung slightly off-center on a clean painted plaster wall in a quiet artist's atelier. The artwork is viewed from a natural standing height, through an offset three-quarter corridor angle. The long wall plane recedes naturally into the distance on the right, creating a clear vanishing direction for the architectural passage. The floor is polished concrete. Subtle studio traces like faint pin marks or minimal paint splatters are visible on the wall, kept secondary to the artwork. Soft, consistent north light streams from a large metal-framed window on the left, illuminating the space and casting gentle shadows. The artwork is relatively close to the viewer, but the architectural elements (floor lines, wall joints) clearly recede, giving depth. No visible people, no animals.

Use an offset three-quarter corridor angle, with the artwork deliberately off-center and close rather than symmetrically framed. Place the artwork on a long wall plane rather than a short frontal display wall. The wall should extend laterally into the scene like a gallery passage, corridor, or elongated architectural plane, creating a clear vanishing direction. Use floor lines, ceiling lines, wall joints, lighting tracks, windows, columns, doorways, or distant wall planes to reinforce depth. The artwork may sit off-center and relatively close to the viewer, while the architecture recedes naturally to one side. Avoid product-style centered symmetry.

contextual_proposals.context_role: Elevates the artwork within a highly curated, architecturally significant private collection, showcasing its presence in a refined design environment.
contextual_proposals.space_type: Refined Midcentury Architect's Loft Living Space
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: Warm, contemplative, art-focused, sophisticated midcentury modern design with a subtle architectural edge and a sense of intimate depth.
materials: Polished concrete floor with warm undertones
Dark wood and tan leather (midcentury lounge chair)
Deep burgundy or terracotta-toned textile accents
contextual_proposals.lighting: Controlled warm daylight filtering through a large window, complemented by subtle, focused track lighting directly illuminating the artwork, creating soft shadows and highlighting textural details.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Borgoña Recovecos / Cámara 3/4 en Loft de Hormigón: Camera height is human but tucked into an architectural recess, approximately 120-160 cm depending on the loft edge. Use a 3/4 view from a recess, corner, or architectural edge, with a burgundy-toned spatial mood and one clean vanishing direction. The artwork is partially contextualized by loft recesses and concrete architecture, creating depth and intimacy without hiding the piece; allow strong off-center framing through recesses, corners, side planes, or architectural edges.
contextual_proposals.camera_distance: Controlled architectural distance, allowing the artwork to dominate while revealing elegant midcentury design elements and architectural depth.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The 3/4 view from an architectural recess or corner emphasizes the artwork's physical presence and depth. The perspective highlights the clean lines of the midcentury interior and the subtle interplay of planes, making the artwork feel organically integrated into the collector's space, consistent with the art-first approach.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: The artwork's stark geometric forms and vibrant colors resonate with the clean lines and material honesty of midcentury design. Placing it in a carefully designed loft highlights its architectural quality and its capacity to anchor a sophisticated, art-conscious interior, appealing to a collector's sensibility for both form and color. The deep green of the artwork is beautifully complemented by rich wood and neutral tones, while the red acts as a powerful focal point, enhanced by the camera's spatial mood.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: This setting positions the artwork as a premium, museum-quality piece suitable for highly curated private and public collections. It appeals to buyers with an appreciation for both modern art and classic 20th-century design, expanding its perceived value and placement potential in high-end residential and commercial spaces. The art-first approach within a desirable design context, enriched by the specific camera's perspective and mood, enhances its market appeal.
contextual_proposals.mockup_prompt: A large, abstract artwork featuring bold green background with a dominant vibrant red vertical rectangle and smaller red/blue forms, hanging on a warm neutral plaster wall within a refined midcentury architect's loft living space. The camera is positioned at human height (120-160 cm) from a 3/4 view, tucked into an architectural recess or corner, revealing concrete architecture elements, with a subtle burgundy-toned spatial mood and one clean vanishing direction. The artwork is off-center, framed by elegant walnut wood paneling on an adjacent wall and the receding lines of the loft, creating depth and intimacy. A low-slung, collectible midcentury modern lounge chair made of dark wood and tan leather, and a minimalist brass and smoked glass side table, are subtly visible in the foreground, supporting the artwork without competing. The floor is polished concrete with a warm undertone. Controlled warm daylight filters through a large window, and focused track lighting subtly illuminates the artwork. No visible people, no animals, artwork intact.
